verse_specific_anchors: avināśi (preserved verbatim, named as the protocol's imperishability)
yena sarvam idaṁ tatam (the verse's central claim — quoted verbatim by the staff engineer in the scenario)
verdict_reason: I tested against neighbors. BG 2.16 cuts between sat/asat at the level of discrimination — the analog there is signal-audit, not substrate. BG 2.18 LICENSES action because the body is perishable — the analog there is migration, not pervasion. BG 2.19 negates agency — the analog there is deprecation grief, not pervasion. BG 2.20 names the substrate as ancient — the analog there is requirement-predates-codebase, not pervasion-of-implementation-by-protocol. The 'protocol pervades implementation' move is verse-17-specific because it anchors on tatam (pervaded), which is the unique grammatical move of this verse in the substrate block. PASS.
